title: ulmus glabra subsp. scabra
intro: Ulmus glabra subsp. scabra, also known as the wavy-leaved elm, is a subspecies of elm tree native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that can help soothe skin irritations and reduce swelling. The bark, leaves, and inner bark of Ulmus glabra subsp. scabra have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, coughs, and digestive problems, due to its antimicrobial and astringent properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a versatile remedy for promoting overall well-being and supporting the body's natural healing processes.
